danger_signals.visual_form: Williams and Roper character sprites with distinctive clothing (yellow vest with camouflage, white vest and bandanna)
danger_signals.meaning: Shadow Warriors gang members—direct combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon sprites (dao blades, improvised weapons) held by Baker-type enemies and armed variants of common foes
danger_signals.meaning: Increased damage potential; heightened danger level in current area

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct character designs (Steve in suit, Jackson as heavyweight boxer, Chin brothers in colored vests, Duke with flashy martial arts style)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat encounter; stage-end confrontation or recurring sub-boss

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive stage themes (casino, airport, dojo, moving truck, city slum, forest, hideout)
danger_signals.meaning: Player advancing deeper into Shadow Warriors territory; escalating threat proximity

safety_signals.visual_form: Dropped weapons from defeated enemies that can be collected and used by player
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for player empowerment; enemy defeat yields tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible UI gauge below life bar that fills when holding L/R buttons
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and preparation; ability to build power before engaging threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard button press mechanic allowing blocking and arm-grab setup
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive readiness possible; player skill-dependent protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Billy Lee with red hair and blue outfit vs. Jimmy Lee with blond flat-top and different outfit
safety_signals.meaning: Player choice and character specialization; tactical flexibility in combat approach

faction_indicators.color_identity: Mixed (yellow vests for Williams type, white for Roper type, varied boss colors)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Williams (blond hair, yellow vest, camouflage pants); Roper (black hair, bandanna, white vest/pants); Baker (armed with dao blades); Jeff (palette swap with darker skin and green clothing); sub-bosses and bosses with specialized martial arts styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hideout location (Mission 7); controlled territories across casino, airport, dojo, city slum, and forest areas

faction_indicators.color_identity: Billy: blue outfit with red hair; Jimmy: distinct outfit with blond flat-top
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mobile through Shadow Warriors territories; not territorially bound

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game / Shadow Warriors expansion phase
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Casino and airport stages feature common enemy types (Williams, Roper) with occasional weapon pickups
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard patrol and attack behavior; basic threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dojo and moving truck stages introduce specialized enemies (Baker with dao blades) and skilled sub-bosses (Jeff, Chin brothers with dual specializations)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More complex attack patterns; enemies with grab mechanics; defensive techniques demonstrated

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game / Approach to power center
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City slum and forest stages feature sustained combat with varied enemy types; environment becomes more chaotic/naturalistic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density; sub-bosses reappear in harder variants

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mission 7 hideout stage with final boss Duke using flashy martial arts style distinct from other foes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-tier combat mechanics; culmination of threat progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Seven-stage journey through casino → airport → dojo → moving truck → city slum → forest → hideout visually maps player's invasion of Shadow Warriors domain
Common enemies (Williams/Roper) establish gang presence; specialized sub-bosses (Jeff, Chin brothers) in mid-stages show organizational hierarchy; final boss Duke represents ultimate authority
Chin brothers' dual specialization (Ron Foo in yellow vest/kicks vs. Ron Pyo in red vest/punches) visually communicates organizational structure and combat philosophy
Urban settings (casino, airport, dojo, slum) transition to wilderness (forest) before hideout, suggesting gang infrastructure embedded in civilian spaces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can be disarmed; weapon becomes player pickup upon defeat
Cannot be disarmed; uses single-sword slashes and helicopter spins; weapons unobtainable by player
Uses most same techniques as Billy Lee including hurricane kick; indicates infiltration of similar martial arts styles into gang ranks
Specializes in kicks; can grab player's kicks and twist leg; operates from Chinatown Kenpo gym
Specializes in punches; can grab player's kicks and twist leg; operates from Chinatown Kenpo gym
Can block attacks with stomach; clown-like appearance suggests physical toughness despite comedic presentation
Giant stature; uses middle kick feared as 'Death Leg'; represents final territorial barrier
Uses flashy martial arts style; exclusive to final encounter; represents Shadow Warriors leadership
Can block enemy punch and trigger arm-grab vulnerability state on Williams, Roper, Steve, Jackson, and Chin brothers
Regular attacks gain extra strength for limited duration; indicates temporary power amplification and tactical window

notes: Super Double Dragon communicates danger primarily through enemy sprite design and stage progression rather than narrative exposition. The game's development notes reveal that narrative context—investigating disappearances, childhood friend revelation—was planned but cut due to time constraints, leaving visual design as the primary world-state communicator. The gang's territorial control is implied through stage progression and enemy density rather than explicit faction markers. Character differentiation (Billy's agility-focused Southern So-Setsu-Ken vs. Jimmy's strength-focused Northern So-Setsu-Ken) is mechanically expressed but not visually obvious beyond outfit/hairstyle differences.
